TranslationContext: This file contains Unit Names for the Elven Conclaves Faction.

Translations.Comment: Concilios conveys the organized, council-like nature of Elven society. Plural maintains the faction's multiple assemblies.
Lanceros Élficos (Elven Spearmen) conveys disciplined infantry wielding spears. Direct and martial.
Guardia de la Ciudad is the traditional term for city guard. Conveys civic and defensive duty.
Maestros de Armas directly conveys mastery and elite martial skill. Fitting for Elven champions.
Arqueros Élficos captures the legendary archery skill of Elves. Direct and evocative.
Centinelas (sentries/watchers) implies vigilance and protection. Solemn and fitting for elven scouts.
Lanzador de Proyectiles (projectile thrower) captures the siege weapon nature. Specific and clear.
Caballería Lancera (lance cavalry) conveys mounted elite troops. Elegant compound for cavalry distinction.
Saqueadores (reavers/raiders) conveys the raiding nature of fast cavalry. Direct and aggressive tone.
Carro de Guerra (war chariot) is classic military terminology. Majestic and fitting for Elven forces.
Águila Gigante is direct and majestic. Captures the nobility of this flying creature fitting for Elven forces.
Caballeros Dragón conveys elite dragon-riding warriors. Elegant compound that captures both mount and rider prestige.
Dragón Ancestral emphasizes age, wisdom, and primordial power. More evocative than simply 'old dragon'.
Grifo is the standard Spanish term for gryphon/griffin. Mythological creature with majesty and power.
Noble Élfico is the traditional term for an aristocratic leader. Conveys grace, authority, and heritage.
Mago Élfico is the standard term for mage/wizard in Spanish fantasy. Direct and recognized in genre.
Hoja Mágica (magical blade) conveys the martial-magical hybrid warrior. Alternative: Espadamago. Elegant and evocative.
